title: cannotCalculateChanges for Space/changes is honest and correct
description: The old Space/changes returned all current space IDs as created[] when sinceState differed. This was a lie: clients following RFC 8620 §5.2 would build a cache that believed all spaces were newly created on every refresh. The new cannotCalculateChanges + newState forces clients to fall back to Space/get with ids=null, which is honest. Resist any suggestion to 'restore' the old behavior for 'compatibility' — clients depending on the old behavior were broken by design.
close_reason: Fixed in code; decision records documented
